summary: Advanced the stage granime decomp in src/melee/gr/granime.c, adding concrete C for grAnime_801C752C, grAnime_801C7C1C, and grAnime_801C8578 while improving nearby matching for grAnime_801C6A54, grAnime_801C7228, and grAnime_801C86D4. The PR also replaced a dont_inline pragma with _inner/_noinline inline-wrapper matching helpers, typed stage archive animation-array fields in UnkStageDat_x8_t, and tightened granime.h prototypes. The PR body and human review comments were empty; decomp.dev reported 3 new 100% matches and 3 unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.change: Main decomp work: +294/-59 lines; implemented and improved granime animation helpers, added inline wrapper tactics, variadic callback handling, archive animation selection, and assert macro use.
Updated prototypes for grAnime_801C752C from loose varargs to HSD_JObj*, s32, s32, void*, u32, ... and grAnime_801C8578 from void* placeholders to HSD_Joint*, s32*.
Replaced padding in UnkStageDat_x8_t at +4/+8/+C with typed animation-array fields: HSD_AnimJoint** unk4, HSD_MatAnimJoint** unk8, HSD_ShapeAnimJoint** unkC.
Added checked target list for granime functions including grAnime_801C86D4, 801C84A4, 801C8578, 801C6A54, 801C752C, 801C6C0C, 801C7228, and 801C7C1C.

what_changed: Implemented grAnime_801C752C as a variadic HSD_JObj animation-callback traversal helper. It builds a callbackArg from AOBJ_ARG_* type cases, applies grAnime_801C6F50/grAnime_801C70E0 to JObj/DObj/RObj paths, optionally recurses through children via grAnime_801C7228, and uses va_start/va_arg/va_end.

Implemented grAnime_801C7C1C to pull HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint arrays from grDatFiles_801C6330(map_id), gate them by arg3 bits, apply or load animations depending on arg5, request animation by flags, set animation rate, and optionally set flags from archive x28 effect flags.
Implemented grAnime_801C8578 as a recursive HSD_Joint child/next traversal driven by a decrementing s32 counter, and changed its header signature to HSD_Joint*, s32*.
Refactored grAnime_801C7228 by extracting repeated object/aobj flag logic into inline grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped and calling grAnime_801C706C_noinline where the diff needed a call rather than inlining.

Added static inline _inner and _noinline wrappers for grAnime_801C67A8, 801C683C, 801C6960, 801C69FC, 801C6A54, 801C706C, and 801C8578; removed the local #pragma dont_inline push/pop around grAnime_801C67A8.

Improved grAnime_801C86D4 frame/scheduling by replacing the local anonymous struct with scalar s32 stack locals, using HSD_ASSERT, loading the archive root joint into a scoped local, and calling grAnime_801C8578_noinline.
Replaced several explicit __assert blocks in granime.c with HSD_ASSERT or HSD_ASSERTREPORT line-number macro forms, and added baselib/debug.h, stdarg.h, and dolphin/os.h includes.
Converted archive animation access in grAnime_801C8138 from base-plus-offset casts to named fields archive->unk4->unk8[arg1].unk4/unk8/unkC.

target_funcs.txt lists grAnime_801C84A4 and grAnime_801C6C0C among completed targets, but the shown diff does not add body changes for those functions and the bot report did not list them as new matches.
smart_moves: The squash commit message explicitly calls out the inline-depth trick: use func_noinline/_inner inline wrappers instead of #pragma dont_inline to keep specific callees emitted as bl calls.

Using wrappers let grAnime_801C683C inline into grAnime_801C6A54 while preserving standalone call shape for grAnime_801C67A8 and grAnime_801C683C; the commit message says this improved grAnime_801C6A54 from 63% to 88% before later final results.
Typing UnkStageDat_x8_t fields turned raw byte-offset casts into normal field loads, making grAnime_801C7C1C and grAnime_801C8138 source cleaner and less brittle.

The shared grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped helper avoided duplicating the same assert/aobj/flags gate in grAnime_801C7228 and grAnime_801C752C, which is useful when source shape matters for repeated assembly patterns.
The grAnime_801C752C implementation handled varargs by AOBJ_ARG_* categories and used f64 for float varargs, matching C default argument promotion rather than forcing f32.
The grAnime_801C86D4 rewrite used a small noinline wrapper and local scope changes to target frame/scheduling rather than relying only on padding.
decomp_lessons: When MWCC inlining control is too coarse, a static inline function plus an _inner/_noinline trampoline can preserve required bl calls without a file-scope #pragma dont_inline.

For variadic animation callbacks, first reconstruct the callback argument ABI: floats arrive as f64 in va_arg, pointer and u32 cases need separate handling, and the downstream code may expect a pointer to a callbackArg union.
Struct/header cleanup can be a matching tactic, not just readability: replacing padding with typed fields can eliminate casts and produce source closer to the original access pattern.
Do not rename unknown archive fields beyond the evidence. This PR improved types for unk4/unk8/unkC but kept generic unk-style names because semantics were not fully established.
Small stack-frame and scheduling changes can matter late in a match; grAnime_801C86D4 moved from a local struct to scalar locals and a scoped root variable before reaching a bot-reported 100%.
Use project assert macros with original line numbers when matching assert-heavy code; this PR replaced manual __assert forms with HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
Watch non-function sections after matching wins: decomp.dev reported a .sdata regression from 71.43% to 47.06% even though granime function matching improved.
naming_conventions: Primary functions retained the address-based project convention grAnime_801Cxxxx because no semantic names were established in the evidence.
Matching helper wrappers used the explicit suffix pattern grAnime_801Cxxxx_inner and grAnime_801Cxxxx_noinline.
The extracted flags/aobj helper was named grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped, signaling that it wraps behavior around grAnime_801C6F50 rather than representing a known original symbol.
Archive struct fields remained generic but offset-oriented: unk0, unk4, unk8, unkC, x28.
Header prototypes were updated from void* placeholders to concrete HSD_Joint*, HSD_JObj*, HSD_AnimJoint*, HSD_MatAnimJoint*, HSD_ShapeAnimJoint*, s32/u32/f32 types when the diff supported them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced #pragma dont_inline with inline-wrapper trampolines to influence whether calls became bl or were inlined.
Marked grAnime_801C6960 and grAnime_801C6F50_wrapped inline, then called them through noinline wrappers where a call boundary was needed.
Removed PAD_STACK(16) from grAnime_801C6A54 after the wrapper strategy changed its match characteristics.
Used noinline wrappers for grAnime_801C67A8, 801C683C, 801C6960, 801C69FC, 801C6A54, 801C706C, and 801C8578 as targeted codegen controls.
Changed grAnime_801C86D4 local layout from an anonymous five-s32 struct to scalar stack locals and scoped archive-root access to tune frame/scheduling.
Replaced raw offset loads with named struct fields to control generated loads while improving readability.
review_feedback: No human PR body, reviews, or review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported 3 new 100% matches: grAnime_801C752C (+560 bytes), grAnime_801C8578 (+348 bytes), and grAnime_801C86D4 (+11 bytes, 93.79% to 100.00%).
decomp.dev reported improvements for unmatched items: grAnime_801C7C1C from 0.00% to 83.11%, grAnime_801C6A54 from 59.00% to 99.23%, and grAnime_801C7228 from 90.10% to 97.72%.
decomp.dev also reported one unmatched-item regression: main/melee/gr/granime .sdata from 71.43% to 47.06%.

The squash commit message documents the rationale for replacing dont_inline pragmas with inline wrappers and for using named UnkStageDat_x8_t fields; there is no separate reviewer discussion in the provided data.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match grAnime_801C7C1C after this 83.11% improvement.
Search for other uses of _inner/_noinline wrapper trampolines replacing #pragma dont_inline in melee decomp.
Check whether the .sdata regression reported for main/melee/gr/granime was fixed in a later PR.
Look for later semantic renames of UnkStageDat_x8_t.unk4, unk8, and unkC beyond their typed animation-array forms.
Inspect AOBJ_ARG_* and callbackArg definitions when continuing grAnime_801C752C or related animation callback work.
